summary: Removed the misleading trailing Archive/Delete swipe actions from expanded Home protocol rows because users could swipe what visually reads like a protocol item/step and archive the entire protocol template. Protocol templates still archive/restore from the explicit Edit Protocol sheet, and template deletion now also lives there as an explicit destructive template action. True per-step archive was not implemented because protocol template steps are plain strings and have no per-step identity/archive state.
validations_passed: xcodebuild build -quiet -project owlory_xcode/Owlory.xcodeproj -scheme Owlory -configuration Debug -destination 'generic/platform=iOS Simulator' -derivedDataPath /tmp/owlory-home-protocol-archive-swipe-build CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO

contract_status_changes.before: The expanded protocol DisclosureGroup owned trailing swipe actions for Archive and Delete. In the visible expanded UI this could read like an action on a protocol item/step, but it archived or deleted the whole protocol template.
contract_status_changes.after: Expanded protocol rows keep the leading Edit swipe only. Template archive/restore and delete are explicit actions in the Edit Protocol sheet. This preserves existing template-level archive/delete behavior while removing the misleading row-level destructive affordance.

residual_risks: True per-step archive remains unresolved. Protocol template steps are currently stored as plain strings, so supporting per-step archive would require a separate model, persistence, migration, and UI slice.
This slice was build-tested, not running-app or screenshot verified. The affordance change should be visually checked in Home before claiming screenshot/device/TestFlight proof.

The supervisor dry run was initially blocked by unrelated untracked browser-harness/ outside the selected slice boundary; this slice proceeded manually inside the selected allowed paths and did not touch that directory.

Pushing the local commit was blocked by the repo pre-push provenance hook because browser-harness/ remains untracked. The slice commit is local until that unrelated workspace dirt is resolved or explicitly ignored by its owner.
The build emitted an existing TodayView iOS 17 onChange deprecation warning unrelated to this Home protocol affordance slice.
recommended_next_reason: Only if per-step archive is still desired: first decide whether protocol template steps need stable identity, archive state, persistence migration, and separate UI semantics. Do not bolt per-step archive onto plain string steps.
git_mirror_status: 1 0 after local commit; push blocked by unrelated untracked browser-harness/
